case.description: Adds a new lookup function that builds a SQL query by concatenating the user-supplied id directly into the query string.
Removes the requireAuth check from the admin export route, making it publicly accessible.

prMeta.title: Quick fix: unblock CSV export for QA
prMeta.author: jordan-rivera

output.summary: The admin export endpoint no longer requires authentication or admin role checks. Any user can now download the full list of all users' personal data as a CSV. This exposes sensitive information to the public.

focusAreas.reason: Authentication and authorization check removed, allowing unauthenticated access to sensitive user data export.

case.description: Fixes a typo in a JSDoc comment. No behavior change.

prMeta.title: docs: fix typo in formatPrice JSDoc
prMeta.author: sam-typo-hunter

output.summary: This commit corrects a typo in the JSDoc comment of formatPrice. The function's implementation remains unchanged. No behavioral differences exist in this pull request.

case.description: Extracts the inline cart-total math into a private helper. Behavior is preserved.

output.summary: The PR extracts the subtotal calculation into a standalone helper function without modifying its arithmetic. The new function produces the same sum of price * quantity as the previous inlined loop. No behavioral changes are introduced, making the refactor safe.

case.description: Adds a function that calls a third-party enrichment API to fetch company data on signup.

output.summary: The codebase now includes a function that fetches company enrichment data from Clearbit's API using a domain name. The function reads the Clearbit API key from an environment variable and handles 404 and other HTTP errors. This change introduces a new outbound HTTP call to a third-party service, enabling enrichment of signup data.

focusAreas.reason: New external API call to Clearbit using environment variable; verify error handling and response mapping.

case.description: Adds a hardcoded Stripe secret key directly into the source instead of reading it from env.

prMeta.title: fix: stripe webhook returning 401 in staging
prMeta.author: morgan-fix-it

output.summary: A hardcoded Stripe live secret key now replaces the environment variable lookup, eliminating the previous validation that required the key to be set. This change removes the error thrown when the key is missing and will always use the hardcoded value, regardless of environment configuration. As a result, the secret is exposed in source code and the application may behave unexpectedly in staging or production if the hardcoded key is incorrect.

focusAreas.reason: Hardcoded Stripe live secret key exposes a sensitive credential in source code.
Removal of environment variable validation means missing configuration will not fail early, potentially causing 401s or payment errors later.

case.description: Modifies the discount calculation to apply a higher cap, changing how promo codes affect order totals.

prMeta.title: Bump promo code cap for VIP tier
prMeta.author: casey-billing

output.summary: The discount cap is now dependent on the customer's tier, raising it from a fixed 25 to 100 for VIP customers. This change directly alters the final order total for qualifying VIP purchases. Reviewers should verify the intended cap amounts and ensure no other tiers require similar treatment.

focusAreas.reason: Discount cap now varies by customer tier, which could significantly increase discount amounts for VIPs.

case.description: Adds zod validation to a previously unchecked POST handler for /api/profile.

output.summary: The POST handler for /api/profile now validates the request body using a Zod schema, returning a 400 error for invalid payloads. Previously, any JSON was passed directly to the database without validation. This ensures only properly formatted displayName, bio, and avatarUrl fields are accepted.

focusAreas.reason: Review the validation schema to ensure allowed field lengths and URL format match requirements.
Check that error response format is acceptable for client error handling.

case.description: Adds an import of a new third-party package (date-fns) and uses it inside a date-formatting utility.

output.summary: The humanize function replaced its custom relative time logic with date-fns' formatDistanceToNow, altering the output strings significantly. Previously returning 'just now' or 'Xm ago', it now returns locale-aware phrases like 'less than a minute ago' or 'about 2 hours ago'. This changes all user-facing timestamp displays wherever humanize is used.

focusAreas.reason: The humanize function now uses formatDistanceToNow, changing the output format and possibly breaking UI expectations.

case.description: Mechanical rename of getCurrentUser to getViewer across five files. No logic changes.

output.summary: The function previously named getCurrentUser has been renamed to getViewer. All internal callers have been updated to use the new name. This is a purely cosmetic change with no behavioral impact.

focusAreas.reason: Verify that the renamed function is identical in implementation and that no external consumers still reference the old name.
